page: Microsoft Azure D4alds_v7 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine belonging to the Daldsv7 server family. Powered by an AMD processor and managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance features 4 dedicated virtual CPUs (vCPUs) and 8.0 GB of system memory. This configuration yields a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU, positioning it as a low-memory, compute-aligned option within its family.

The server includes 236 GB of local storage and is premium storage capable, allowing integration with high-performance external disk options. It does not feature any graphics processing units (GPUs) or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The inclusion of local storage provides a fast temporary disk space for scratch files or caching, while the premium storage capability supports workloads requiring consistent disk performance.

Due to its low-memory profile, the D4alds_v7 offers a cost-efficient alternative for workloads that require dedicated CPU resources but do not demand large memory footprints. This resource density makes it suitable for compute-focused tasks with low memory requirements, such as web serving, lightweight application servers, batch processing, and basic computational tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure D4alds_v7 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine from the Daldsv7 family, running on an AMD processor under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs and 8.0 GB of system memory, resulting in a low-memory configuration of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance is equipped with 236 GB of local storage and supports premium storage options, though it lacks GPU acceleration and complimentary public IPv4 addresses. By pairing dedicated compute resources with a streamlined memory profile, this server provides a cost-efficient solution for workloads that are not memory-intensive. It is suited for tasks such as web serving, lightweight application hosting, batch processing, and entry-level computational workloads.
